skill.name: lead-magnets
skill.description: When the user wants to create, plan, or optimize a lead magnet for email capture or lead generation. Also use when the user mentions "lead magnet," "gated content," "content upgrade," "downloadable," "ebook," "cheat sheet," "checklist," "template download," "opt-in," "freebie," "PDF download," "resource library," "content offer," "email capture content," "Notion template," "spreadsheet template," or "what should I give away for emails." Use this for planning what to create and how to distribute it. For interactive tools as lead magnets, see free-tool-strategy. For writing the actual content, see copywriting. For the email sequence after capture, see email-sequence.
skill.summary: Plan and optimize lead magnets for email capture and lead generation with format-specific guidance and benchmarks.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections refer to marketing tool names (Beacon.by, Designrr) and YAML metadata, not actual cryptography. 'System reconnaissance' findings are marketing terms like SEO in documentation context. The skill is purely instructional marketing content with no executable code or security risks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Marketing Tool Names Flagged
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Beacon.by' and 'Designrr' (legitimate marketing tools) as suspicious. These are ebook creation and link management tools referenced in format-guide.md line 36. No actual security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct verification: Beacon.by is a legitimate link-in-bio tool, Designrr is an ebook creation platform. Context is clearly marketing documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Code Formatting Detected as Execution
low_findings.description: Line 15 in SKILL.md uses backticks for markdown code formatting to display file paths. Static scanner incorrectly interpreted this as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is documentation text, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms markdown formatting, not code execution. File contains only instructional text with code examples in backticks.

content.value_statement: Plan lead magnets that capture emails and generate qualified leads. Get format-specific guidance for checklists, templates, ebooks, quizzes, and webinars with benchmarks and examples.
actual_capabilities: Recommend lead magnet formats based on target audience and buyer stage (awareness, consideration, decision)
Provide structure templates for checklists, cheat sheets, templates, swipe files, ebooks, mini-courses, quizzes, and webinars
Suggest gating strategies (full gate, partial gate, ungated) with form field recommendations
Outline landing page structure and delivery methods (instant download, email delivery, thank you page)
Plan distribution channels including blog CTAs, exit-intent popups, social media, paid ads, and partner co-promotion
Define KPIs and benchmarks for measuring lead magnet performance with conversion rate expectations by format
limitations: Does not write actual lead magnet content—use copywriting skill for content creation
Does not implement interactive tools like calculators or graders—use free-tool-strategy skill for technical implementation
Does not design landing page visuals or create graphic assets
Benchmark data is general guidance—actual performance varies by audience, niche, and traffic quality

use_cases.title: SaaS Product Launch
use_cases.description: Create a lead magnet to capture emails for a new SaaS product. The skill helps select the right format (e.g., template or checklist) based on the target buyer stage and outlines distribution through existing blog content and paid channels.
use_cases.target_user: Product marketing manager launching B2B software

use_cases.title: Agency Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Develop gated content to generate qualified leads for a marketing agency. The skill provides format recommendations for high-value offers like industry reports or assessment tools that demonstrate expertise and attract decision-makers.
use_cases.target_user: Agency owner or marketing consultant building client pipeline

use_cases.title: Content Creator Email List
use_cases.description: Grow an email list for a creator or educator. The skill suggests quick-win lead magnets like checklists or templates that can be created in hours, with promotion strategies for social media and audience building.
use_cases.target_user: Solo creator, blogger, or course creator growing audience

prompt_templates.title: Recommend Lead Magnet Format
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a lead magnet for [product/service]. My target audience is [ICP description]. What lead magnet format would work best for [buyer stage: awareness/consideration/decision] stage?
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs initial recommendation for lead magnet type and format

prompt_templates.title: Create Content Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed outline for a [format: checklist/template/ebook/cheat sheet] about [topic]. Include [number] key sections and specify what makes each valuable.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has selected a format and needs structured content outline

prompt_templates.title: Plan Distribution Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a [format] about [topic]. Where should I promote it to reach [target audience]? I have [existing traffic sources/assets].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs promotion and distribution channel recommendations

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Conversion Rates
prompt_templates.prompt: My lead magnet landing page converts at [current rate]%. I'm targeting [target rate]%. What should I test? Current setup: [describe current headline, preview, form fields, CTA].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has live lead magnet and wants A/B testing ideas to improve performance

output_examples.input: I sell an SEO tool for small businesses. What lead magnet should I create?
output: - Naturally leads to your product (checklist reveals problems your tool solves)
1. Technical SEO Basics (meta tags, headers, sitemap)
2. On-Page Optimization (keywords, content quality, internal links)
3. Site Performance (page speed, mobile-friendliness, broken links)
4. Content Audit (thin content, duplicate content, orphan pages)
5. Local SEO (Google Business Profile, citations, reviews)
- Full gate (high perceived value of checklist)
- Blog post: 'The 15-Point SEO Checklist Every Small Business Needs'
- Exit-intent popup on SEO-related blog posts
- Social media: Share 3 checklist items as teaser
- Facebook/Instagram lead ads targeting small business owners

output_examples.input: Help me create a template lead magnet for project managers
output: - Include 'How to Use' tab with instructions
- Use conditional formatting for overdue tasks (red)
- Add dropdown menus for status (Not Started/In Progress/Done)
- Lock formula cells, leave input cells editable
- Provide 'Make a Copy' link for easy duplication
- Headline: 'Manage Any Project in Minutes—Free Google Sheets Template'
- Preview: Screenshot of the template dashboard
- What's inside: 4 templates included, fully customizable
- Form: Email only, CTA 'Send Me the Templates'
- Instant download on thank you page

best_practices: Match lead magnet format to buyer stage—awareness leads need education (guides, ebooks), consideration leads need comparison tools (templates, assessments), decision leads need implementation help (templates, checklists)
Ask for minimum information in capture forms—email-only converts best, each additional field reduces conversion by 5-10%
Create lead magnets consumable in under 30 minutes—high perceived value with low time investment drives higher completion and satisfaction rates
anti_patterns: Creating broad 'all-in-one' guides instead of specific problem-solving content—specific checklists and templates outperform generic comprehensive guides
Gating low-value content without preview—show table of contents, first page, or sample results to demonstrate value before requiring email
Promoting lead magnets without context—match the offer to the page content (content upgrades convert 2-5x better than generic sidebar CTAs)

faq.question: What lead magnet format converts best?
faq.answer: Checklists and cheat sheets typically convert highest (30-50%) due to low commitment and immediate value perception. Templates also convert well (25-45%) and attract higher-quality leads. Ebooks convert lower (20-35%) but build more authority. Choose format based on both conversion goals and lead quality needs.

faq.question: Should I gate my entire lead magnet or offer a preview?
faq.answer: Full gating maximizes email capture but reduces reach. Partial gating (showing preview like table of contents or first chapter) balances both. For top-funnel content, consider ungating with optional email capture. Content upgrades (bonuses specific to blog posts) work best fully gated since context is already established.

faq.question: How do I deliver lead magnets effectively?
faq.answer: Best practice: thank you page with instant download plus email delivery. This provides immediate gratification while verifying the email address. For email courses or webinars, use email-only delivery with clear timeline expectations. Always include a next-step CTA on the thank you page.

faq.question: What's a realistic conversion rate for lead magnet landing pages?
faq.answer: Warm traffic (blog readers, social followers): 20-40%. Cold traffic (paid ads, organic search): 5-15%. Exit-intent popups convert 2-5% of visitors. Content upgrades (contextual blog post offers) convert 3-8% of post readers. Your results vary based on offer relevance, audience match, and page optimization.

faq.question: How long should it take to create a lead magnet?
faq.answer: Checklists and cheat sheets: 1-4 hours. Templates: 2-8 hours. Swipe files: 4-8 hours. Ebooks: 1-3 weeks. Quizzes: 1-2 weeks. Webinar prep: 1 week. Start with quick wins (checklists, templates) to validate demand before investing in ebooks or courses.

faq.question: Can this skill help me build interactive lead magnets like calculators?
faq.answer: No—for interactive tools like calculators, graders, and quizzes, use the free-tool-strategy skill. This lead-magnets skill focuses on static formats (PDFs, templates, documents) and strategic planning. Interactive tools require technical implementation guidance covered in free-tool-strategy.
